programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. Meanwhile, Bishop and her husband invite the team out for a special dinner. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

Tony gets involved in police work while moonlighting as a cabbie - to Dan Casper's amusement

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis Hardy heroically rescues a man from a burning building, while partner Roger Valentine returns to duty only to suffer a panic attack when he's faced with a crisis. Suzie Sim passes her exam and starts looking for a new post, and Adam Okaro sets his sights on becoming Borough Commander, but is annoyed to find he has tough competition

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Two girls go missing in circumstances that echo the Amy Tennant case and Mickey Webb is horrified to find he knows the fretful parents. Yvonne and Laura find the girls, beaten but alive, and as six-year-old Molly is rushed into intensive care, her older sister Charlotte gives the desperate CID officers information leading to an arrest. Former EastEnders actress Hannah Waterman guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Molly Parker makes a slow recovery, sister Charlotte makes a shocking confession that prompts Mickey Webb to confront their father with barely concealed fury. James Tennant faces further anguish when it becomes clear Amy's disappearance is not related to the Parker case. Terry Perkins considers building bridges with his estranged family, but ex-wife Helen is not easily impressed

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter, at least on first impressions, but soon changes her opinion when his housekeeper dies suddenly

programme.title: Call the Midwife

Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Social climber Hyacinth indulges another of her flamboyant whims and decides she and Richard need a new car - but a modest three-door hatchback is not what she had in mind. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to share her unparalleled experience of being the perfect hostess by advertising her talents in the local newspaper. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Alexa investigates a case that runs the gamut of birth, marriage and murder when a young man is found dead in an alley after a fight at his brother's wedding. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The Edinburgh detective investigates the murder of notorious gangster Morris Cafferty's illegitimate son, as the search goes on to find the person who put Sammy in a life-threatening coma. The hunt for the killer is complicated by apparent links to a spate of hate crimes by racist extremists in the city centre. Gritty crime thriller, starring John Hannah and James Cosmo

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a wealthy banker is found dead at the bottom of her stairs, suspicion immediately falls on her media personality husband, who claims that she fell. Harry conducts the official post-mortem for the police, but finds himself on opposite sides of the case when Nikki performs a second one for the husband's solicitor - and comes up with very different results. Guest starring John Lynch

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Sequel to The White Queen, set in the Middle Ages and picking up as the tumultuous War of the Roses comes to an end, a new generation ascends to the throne in a tale of power, political clashes, family, love and betrayal. As the victor of the Battle of Bosworth, Henry Tudor, is set to ascend the throne and Elizabeth of York and her family, supporters of the former King, find themselves in grave danger. Lizzie must fulfil her promise to marry King Henry VII and bring peace to the nation. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.desc: A man researching a guidebook on cross-country walks foolishly accepts Foggy's offer of help, hoping the self-proclaimed survival expert's military training ensures his expedition runs like clockwork. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When an old foe tells him that she has arranged the death of her former lover, Father Brown soon finds himself blamed for the murder. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Past and present collide when the sleuth investigates the macabre world of doll's house crime scenes. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian struggles to control his urge to drink and finds refuge in a commune at the centre of the team's investigation into the death of a university student. The case also helps Gerry to face up to his relationship with Emily - who has not spoken to him since she discovered the truth about the DNA test at Ricky Hanson's trial. Police drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jack turns out to have a personal connection to the prime suspect in a teenage girl's murder, so he is taken off the case and replaced with another forensic scientist seconded to the Lyell Centre. But he finds himself instinctively drawn into the investigation, alienating himself from Nikki and the team as he tries to find an alibi for the supposed killer. Meanwhile, the victim's best friend is consumed with guilt, leading her to take drastic action. Crime drama, starring David Caves, Emilia Fox and Richard Lintern, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Ashley Walters

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis and Emma question a man about criminal damage in their first unsupervised interview, and end up with a murder confession. The probationers are further shocked to see the suspect walk free for lack of evidence. Roger is referred to a psychologist to check whether he is fit for duty and Gina bullies Dan and Will into signing up for an inter-service boxing match after learning of their new hobby

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien is forbidden to enter her own home, and appeals to Tracey and Sharon to help solve the problem, but the sisters find the situation too funny to provide any assistance

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: King Henry VII sets out to establish himself as the new sovereign, but finds a dangerous, deeply divided kingdom. Lizzie's loyalties are further complicated by the child she is expecting. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Max gets dragged through the press after Jan's betrayal - but he is offered the perfect chance to redeem himself when he comes to the aid of a patient attacked in the department. A ghost from the past threatens to put a damper on Fin and Comfort's new-found happiness, and Nikki confronts Roxy about the theft. Hospital drama, starring Robert Gwilym, Judy Loe and Derek Thompson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan realises he can't wriggle out of the boxing contest after Gina presents him with a gruelling training schedule, and Will's pursuit of Emma goes a stage further when they visit the pub to celebrate a result on Adam Okaro's new capture car initiative. programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack and Strickland form an unlikely alliance that sees them break the rules to find out why a British soldier died while taking part in a Ministry of Defence medical trial. programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki and Jack are called to a petrol station where three people have been shot dead, and conclude it is the work of a concealed shooter working from long range - a sniper is at large in London. They combine ballistics and wound pathology with the killer-profiling skills of senior investigator DCI Jane De Freitas, as they work to find this seemingly random murderer before panic hits the city. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Zoe Telford and Steve Wall

programme.desc: Jon Gower discovers how the widespread use of cannons rendered castles less useful as defensive positions, leading to them becoming symbols of wealth and shrines to a romantic past

programme.title: Blociau Rhif

programme.title: Octonots
programme.desc: The Octonauts follow emperor penguin mums returning to their families. One of the penguins gets injured and the Octonauts try to carry her home, but are left without radio connection or a map

programme.title: Jamborî
programme.desc: A musical adventure full of interesting sounds and colourful pictures, where shadows dance, robots play and fruits move to the music

programme.title: Sion y Chef
programme.desc: Izzy tries to win the county show's vegetable competition with her giant marrow

programme.title: Ynys Adra
programme.desc: Blogs in which Welsh children reveal how they are spending their time in isolation

programme.title: Pat a Stan
programme.desc: Adventures with the two friends who turn everyday events into crazy adventures

programme.title: Byd Rwtsh Dai Potsh
programme.desc: Dave gets a girlfriend who turns out to be an alien who looks a bit like a familiar household item

programme.title: Y Barf
programme.desc: Villain Arch-Elin is on the loose, and she is trying to stop Welsh children learning poetry. Together with sidekick Noni, Y Barf uses all his might to defeat her. Children's entertainment

programme.title: Arthur a Chriw y Ford Gron
programme.desc: The villagers are scared to venture into the forest ever since a singing dolmen was discovered there

programme.title: Larfa
programme.desc: The gang has an adventure with a car

programme.title: 3 Lle
programme.desc: TV presenter and journalist Sean Fletcher revisits his past, including Widford Lodge School in Chelmsford and Tor Bay in the Gower

programme.title: Stori Pêl-Droed Cymru
programme.desc: Fan and author Dewi Prysor looks at the ways in which clubs and their fans have expressed their identity, from fanzines to songs, and more recently, through social media. He also looks at how support for one's team can sometimes spiral out of control

programme.title: Newyddion S4C
programme.desc: News

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: Magazine programme, featuring stories from all over Wales

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Pobol y Cwm
programme.desc: Lauren Phillips looks back at some of the most memorable stories from Cwmderi involving Kelly Evans

programme.title: Only Men Aloud
programme.desc: The singers perform a selection of their favourite classical music, including excerpts from Nabucco, La Boheme, Fidelio, Madama Butterfly and Turandot, in the company of this week's guest Rhian Lois

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Cefn Gwlad
programme.desc: Dai Jones returns to Hafod y Llan Farm in Snowdonia in February. He meets manager John Till, who leads ramblers up and down Snowdon's slopes and talks about the farm's present and future role

programme.title: Tewach Na Dwr
programme.desc: Oskar waits for news on the identity of the dead body recovered from the sea. Jonna triumphs in the lead role of the local theatre production. The family get a new neighbour

programme.title: Pobol y Rhondda
programme.desc: Rhondda-born Siôn Tomos Owen creates a unique map of the area with portraits of the people he meets. In this episode he herds sheep and samples beer, hears about a family who have lived in the same house for 150 years and steps into the world of a YouTube star

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two expats who want to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is helped by Norman Wilkinson, the Bristol Upholstery Collective and Guy Trench to transform fencing, a pair of demi-johns, a nursing chair and a fire-damaged table

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the extraordinary idea of deploying the Spy Creatures, showing how the concept evolved and became the inspiration for the animatronic animals of the series. This documentary reveals the painstaking work behind building the lifelike models, from first concept until they become 'alive' for the first time. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat House to follow the lives of the exotic animals at its safari park, beginning by looking at the impact of the pandemic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the aid of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also learns about Captain Matthew Webb, the first person to swim the English Channel

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Political, commercial and romantic rivalries are stirred and Mich's friendship with Sabine might not survive the turmoil

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of BBC comedy Still Game take part, with Gavin Mitchell, Sanjeev Kohli, Mark Cox, Paul Young and Jane McCarry taking on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Three people compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs, beginning with sausage factory supervisor Mica, bingo host Rob and head gardener Dan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the country lanes of Northern Ireland in their Rolls-Royce. Angus has designs on a piece of Victorian furniture while Roo hits the dance floor

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Victoria Derbyshire investigates the impact of lockdown for those living with an abusive partner, revealing the scale of domestic violence at the height of the crisis and meeting some of those who managed to escape. She asks if the Government has done enough for those who were put in danger by having to stay at home

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Letitia Dean and Kellie Bright, aka Sharon Watts and Linda Carter, to look back at 35 years of Queen Vic landladies, from Angie Watts to Peggy Mitchell. The actresses also revisit their own biggest moments behind the bar, reveal juicy backstage gossip and tease what's to come as Linda hands the landlady reins back to Sharon - all before Stacey puts their landlady knowledge to the ultimate test

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Fortune smiles on Mike and Alison when a TV company comes calling, looking for an old house in which to film a period drama. But to get the money in the bank, Alison must ensure the shoot goes without a hitch despite the horde of curious ghosts, the house's crumbling floorboards and her crippling crush on the show's lead actor. Meanwhile, Thomas and Lady Button learn the meaning of a free pass, and Julian tries his luck at betting on horse racing. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates goes on the run, convinced he can save his career and marriage by proving his innocence. As his options dwindle, the anti-corruption team faces renewed pressure to close the case - but rifts start to appear when Arnott questions how far Fleming is willing to go to get results. Police thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: As Mustafa's search for his money continues to lead down dead ends, Kazim is busy looking after Jeremiah, who cannot help but reflect on what path he should take next. There are bigger problems for the boys as Mustafa is intent on capturing the Speedi-Kazz team, but help comes from the least expected place

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Allie X is the guest host as the contestants' looks and improv skills are tested, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast presenter Steph McGovern takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with journalist Ash Sarkar and comedian Josh Widdicombe joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A tiger undergoes surgery, but the vets are soon faced with a difficult decision. A cockatoo ruffles some feathers, and the park's white rhinos enjoy a detoxing charcoal spa

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The new owners of a terraced house in London's Plumstead, a former bakery in Whitstable, Kent, and a three-bedroom semi near Derby city centre reveal their plans for the properties

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood gets a taste of life as a police officer in Aransas Pass on the Texas Gulf Coast, an area with a crime rate 90 per cent higher than the rest of the state

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits Castell Coch

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Demands on Max lay him low, and a stolen phone draws Ash and Helena together

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Chris Rogers, Helen Fospero, Ricky Andalcio, Miranda Krestovnikoff and Kevin Duala form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple looking to move to the Norfolk countryside on a budget of £600,000, and volunteers trackside on the world's smallest public railway

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: an estate agent, a street food vendor and a bee farmer compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth traverse south-west Wales, where they learn about traditional boats and sheep farming before attending an auction in Carmarthen

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bonfire night sees fireworks of a different kind in this one-hour special from 2000 which marked the departure of Frank Butcher following the discovery of his affair. As the Vic fills with drunken revellers for Peggy's party, Frank panics when he realises the note he left his wife has gone missing

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Guests Vanessa Williams, Mark Gatiss, Nikkie Tutorials and Denise Van Outen join Michelle for a special episode celebrating her 23rd wedding anniversary, in which we discover how lockdown has affected romance

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode reaches the letter W in his alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive, highlighting Rick Stein's potato and watercress soup, and the Hairy Bikers' white chocolate cheesecake

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Featuring a man in Margate who has come up with a unique way of using a shed roof to grow vegetables, and inspirational gardener Sue Kent in Swansea, who shows how she has designed her plot to enable her to garden successfully despite her upper limb difference. Last year, Joe Swift went to Devon to visit a garden where the planting combinations have been inspired by world travel and in Hampshire a sea captain explains how he has created a low maintenance garden using foliage and structural plants

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter checks up on her chilies and peppers in her countryside garden as well as the cucumbers in her polytunnel, and Kirsty Wilson tries out an easy-to-use propagator to make houseplants for free. Sophie McKilligan returns to her Aberdeen allotment after a break to visit family, and finds the rest of the community have kindly looked after her patch while she was away

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner discusses culinary memories with Anneka Rice, revisiting her childhood in Surrey, and creates two dishes that take her back to that time

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste explore the benefits of nature, with a trip to the woods and a day spent working on a farm

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The duo arrive in Italy, where they abandon their hogs for the gondolas of Venice, and bake a classic focaccia by the side of a canal. They later visit Si King's sister, who lives in Tuscany, and savour a pasta pie in the back streets of Ferrara. There then follows a dash for Turin to help celebrate the country's 150th birthday, a visit to a hazelnut farm to learn the secrets of a certain chocolate spread and the chance to re-create big-screen movie The Italian Job on the rooftop of the old Fiat factory

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage from the third session of the final, as the players face a potential eight frames in the ranking tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield. John Higgins and Judd Trump won four frames apiece in this session last year, which was of greater benefit to Trump, as it allowed him to go into the final session with a 16-9 lead, thus needing just two more frames to claim victory

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy's Secret World of Japanese Bicycle Racing
programme.desc: The Olympic great explores the keirin, one of the most extreme and exciting events in track cycling, examining its origin and popularity in Japan. He makes a return to the country after being invited there for a keirin school in 2005, which is considered a great honour for international cyclists

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness look back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring Freddie trying to bungee-jump a car off a dam, Paddy proving an old hearse can be the ultimate family car, and Chris remembering the iconic Lotus 79 race car

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: All the action from the concluding session of this year's final at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first player to reach 18 frames is crowned champion. Judd Trump needed just two frames to wrap up an impressive 18-9 victory in this session last year, recording breaks of 94 and 63 to comfortably overcome the four-time champion and claim his maiden title. 'The Juddernaut' has won six ranking competitions since to cement his status at the top of the rankings and put him among the pre-tournament favourites to prevail once again. However, former champions including Mark Selby, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Neil Robertson and Mark Williams also entered with high hopes and all made it past the first round making a winner very difficult to call

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fourth day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The police visit Niki in search of information about her fugitive husband DL Hawkins, while Matt puts his mind-reading skills to good use in a bid to improve his marriage. Hiro and Ando find themselves out of their depth in Las Vegas, and Peter receives a life-changing message from a surprising source

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Niki has a surprise visitor and later confronts her mirror image, while Hiro and Ando find their journey to New York delayed when they land themselves in trouble in Las Vegas. Elsewhere, Claire meets her biological parents in an attempt to shed light on her powers, and Hiro sends a vital message to Isaac

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: When Iraq's president Saddam Hussein was captured by coalition forces in December 2003, politicians in the White House believed this would be the turning point they needed to bring democracy to Iraq. This edition looks at how Saddam's removal led to a new chapter of conflict as sectarian differences flared up to plunge Iraq into a civil war. Senior CIA analyst John Nixon was the first person to question Saddam, and the dictator's answers during the five-week interrogation left the American both surprised and horrified

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Guy finds Ric lying unconscious in theatre, and decides to seize the opportunity he is presented with. It falls to Louis and Donna to uncover his intentions and stop him before it is too late. Sacha is encouraged by Fletch to set things right with Essie, only to discover she has big plans for the future. Nicky is hurt by Cameron's odd behaviour towards her, and receives an unexpected offer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein return to their investigation of a toxic love triangle, which culminated in the murder of farm owner Frederick Morton

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich reveal their favourite water features, including a Japanese-inspired pond complete with teahouse

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers take Flower's new pups on their first foraging trip and are caught out in the open by another group of war-dancing meerkats. Meanwhile, two males who want to mate with Lazuli females join forces with Yossarian

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to Java's Central Province and the royal city of Yogyakarta, where he visits the Sultan's Palace and admires the ancient art of shadow puppetry. Moving north to Ambawara, Michael boards a scenic heritage line constructed by the Dutch with carriages dating from the time of his Bradshaw's guidebook. Moving on, he learns how coffee was first brought to Java for cultivation, visits the Great Mosque and ends at Surabaya, a port bursting with local traditions, produce and culture

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons, beginning on New Year's Day, with the landscape in the grip of winter. Time-lapse photography shows a magical country shrouded in frost and swirling mist, while water becomes the enemy as the creatures must cope with ice. Red squirrels resort to subterfuge, kites track a farmer's plough to get at the worms beneath the frost, and as winter fades adders bask in the sun and the woodland floor erupts with snowdrops

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of teams captained by Springwatch presenter Chris Packham and zoologist Lucy Cooke across a variety of intriguing rounds. Iolo Williams and Lizzie Daly are the guests, with every correct answer wins part of a mysterious animal and as the show progresses each team builds its very own bespoke beast that they must correctly identify the parts of in the endgame, while the show is packed with breathtaking natural history clips, extraordinary animal facts and spirited debate

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah clashes with Henry when he misses the symptoms of a diabetic coma in an Aboriginal patient, and Elizabeth's life is turned upside down when Douglas begins coughing up blood

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical journey, this time looking at dishes linked by the letter R. Recipes from the archive include Nigel Slater cooking ribs and Raymond Blanc creating rice pudding

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston uncovers the workings of the human body, looking closely at case studies of individuals with extremely rare conditions. In the first edition, she meets seven-year-old Virsaviya, who was born with her heart on the outside of her ribcage, and Jeannie Peeper, who has a condition that sees her body growing an extra skeleton where there should be muscle

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents this documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, unlocking the secrets behind some of the world's most unusual occurrences. The first episode features a monkey that has learnt first aid and a bird-killing solar death ray

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People in Weston-super-Mare bring their valuables along to the Grand Pier for valuation by experts Jonathan Pratt and Thomas Plant. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers more about the seaside pier itself

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their general knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Lazuli tribe becomes entangled in a three-way conflict when new family the Commandos spends the night in Whiskers territory and arouses the anger of the clan, with tragic results

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fifth day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Our Friend Victoria
programme.desc: Daniel Rigby looks back at Victoria Wood's life on screen and the memorable moments she created on the topic of fame. Clips include her performances on That's Life, sketches from Wood & Walters, and As Seen On TV. There is also footage from Acorn Antiques and contributions from Julie Walters, Mark Gatiss, Michael Ball and Maxine Peake

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The sixth first-round match sees an Oxbridge clash as four students from Balliol College, Oxford pit their wits against a team from Clare College, Cambridge in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Trial of Alex Salmond
programme.desc: In March this year, Scotland's former First Minister Alex Salmond emerged from the High Court in Edinburgh cleared of 14 charges of sexual misconduct, with the verdict concluding one of the most dramatic trials Britain has seen. This programme sees Kirsty Wark travelling across Scotland as she follows the trial and interviews people who were present at key moments surrounding the political scandal, and looking back at Salmond's political career and the growing rift between him and Nicola Sturgeon

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians James Acaster, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Nish Kumar and Zoe Lyons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Comedy short allowing viewers into the weird world of Herbert Clunkerdunk as he speaks directly to the camera - only to be constantly interrupted by his own imagination. Herbert has a big audition, so he gets ready and takes the all-important call from agent before rehearsing his lines. Starring Spencer Jones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: BBC News Arabic uncovers Silicon Valley's illegal online slave market, hosting apps used for buying and selling domestic workers in the Gulf

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in the Cotswolds at the Broadway Tower folly to look back at unusual events that have featured on the programme over the years. Including the time Anita Rani met a farmer growing a crop of chairs, and when Margherita Taylor made microscopic art from nature

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges an abstract artist, an intuitive painter, and a stained glass worker to create a bespoke piece for buyers in Cheddar who want something uplifting

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades revisits three fixes from the barn - a polyphon, gramophone and transistor radio - to tell the story of how modern music has been influenced by vintage music players

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan. Mandy Carter is a woman with big dreams - and today her dream is to find work. But the only thing available to her is a job in a fruit packing factory, where a big mallet will come in handy. With Michelle Greenidge and Tom Basden

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After entering the local line dancing endurance challenge Mandy finds herself up against an old rival. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan, with Maxine Peake

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat House to follow the lives of the exotic animals at its safari park, beginning by looking at the impact of the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Three people compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs, beginning with sausage factory supervisor Mica, bingo host Rob and head gardener Dan

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Lazuli tribe becomes entangled in a three-way conflict when new family the Commandos spends the night in Whiskers territory and arouses the anger of the clan, with tragic results

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes space in the writing garden for plants that will flower later in the season and plants out tender vegetables for late summer harvests. Cameras revisit Frances Tophill's allotment to join her as she demonstrates an unusual method of reducing weeds and also catch up with a gardener in Wales who has dedicated years to breeding some very special varieties of rhododendrons. Plus, Joe Swift heads to Devon to find out about the design and planting challenges faced when creating a coastal garden

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons. Spring marks a series of beginnings, as trees explode with blossom and mornings fill with the chorus of birdsong. Long-tailed tits frantically build nests, a stoat mother hunts rabbits to feed her playful young, while in the oceans, seahorses sway to a graceful courtship dance. As spring becomes summer, guillemot chicks leap from their cliffs to begin life at sea. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from the latest fixtures, including Wigan Warriors v Leeds Rhinos and St Helens v Wakefield Trinity

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Niall Strawson and Ferne Corrigan

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah finds Leah and persuades her to return home to see her uncle, and Douglas continues with strenuous work at the mission despite being told to rest by his doctor

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter L. The Hairy Bikers cook a lasagne and Jamie Oliver bakes a lemon and lime cream tart

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston investigates the human body's ability to adapt for survival, meeting with individuals who push this aptitude to the absolute limit. She meets a man who injects himself with snake venom, a woman living with only half a brain, and the only man in the world to be completely cured of HIV

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, exploring another selection of bizarre stories from across the globe. From his base in Nevada, he uncovers the secret of a suspiciously fishy oil slick, reveals what is making a lemming very angry and finds out about a man who can see with his ears

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Hull, Caroline Hawley is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews, and Nick Hall is dazzled by some jewellery. Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their general knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower decides it is time to move because the Whiskers have been at the same burrow for five weeks. Her pregnancy makes her bad-tempered, which puts her three daughters Kinkajou, Daisy and Mozart on alert and at risk of eviction from the troop

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which famous faces compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge. Hayley Mills and her friend and Wild at Heart co-star Stephen Tompkinson set out on a trip around north-east England in classic cars, receiving expert guidance from Christina Trevanion and David Harper as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at an auction in West Yorkshire. Hayley detours to hear the little-known story behind Earl Grey tea and Stephen visits the home of a famous amateur football team

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Documentary about the people living and working in Manchester's remarkable housing boom. A millionaire developer plans to transform the city's red light district into a fashionable residential quarter, as well as taking charge of organising a fundraising concert for a homelessness charity. In Salford, a single mother finds herself priced out of the area she grew up in, and must find a new home for her two young children

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jack Dee hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by observational comedian Seann Walsh and king of the one-liners Milton Jones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Trial of Alex Salmond
programme.desc: In March this year, Scotland's former First Minister Alex Salmond emerged from the High Court in Edinburgh cleared of 14 charges of sexual misconduct, with the verdict concluding one of the most dramatic trials Britain has seen. This programme sees Kirsty Wark travelling across Scotland as she follows the trial and interviews people who were present at key moments surrounding the political scandal, and looking back at Salmond's political career and the growing rift between him and Nicola Sturgeon

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades traces heroic stories from the Second World War and how they still resonate today, revisiting three fixes from the barn; a watch, swagger stick and belt

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter reveals the container potatoes she planted in the first episode, George Anderson harvests his cabbage and blackcurrants and Chris Beardshaw checks on the progress of his box blight battle and explains the art of cloud pruning

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the fourth day of the Second Test in the three-match series, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Pakistan are looking for their first series victory on English soil since 1996, though they secured draws on each of their two previous visits

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by Stephen Fry for one of his favourite concerts, as Daniel Barenboim conducts his West-Eastern Divan Orchestra in Liszt's Piano Concerto No 1, performed in 2016 by his childhood friend, pianist Martha Argerich, along with epic orchestral performances from three different operas by Richard Wagner

programme.title: Albion
programme.desc: Mike Bartlett's tragi-comic drama about national identity, family, passion and the disappointment of personal dreams, directed for the stage by Rupert Goold. Filmed at London's Almeida Theatre, the play is set in the garden of an English country house, which has been bought by successful businesswoman Audrey Walters (Victoria Hamilton), who intends to restore the ruined garden to its former glory, and to create a memorial to the son she recently lost in a foreign war

programme.title: The Gardeners of Kabul
programme.desc: Five hundred years ago, the Emperor Babur laid out beautiful gardens in the city of Kabul, now the capital of Afghanistan. Cameras follow the gardeners keeping the Emperor's legacy alive

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sean goes to great lengths to convince Roxy he'd be a good father. Chelsea is driven to drink and leads Libby astray, while Bradley's attempts to impress his boss don't go to plan

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sean cleans up his act to prove to Roxy he will be a responsible father. Patrick tries to make amends with Chelsea, and Dot notices the sexual tension between Jack and Tanya

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack struggles with his feelings when he hears Ronnie is leaving Walford. Roxy prepares to escape from Sean's influence, and Jane reluctantly asks for Bianca's help

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sean can't convince Roxy to stay when he catches up with her just as she's leaving the Square, Tanya begins to regret the choice she made over Jack, and Bradley sets out to impress

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inherited knowledge and the meaning of tradition, craftsmanship and artistic processes in three African cultures, beginning with the weaving of a bamboo house in the remote highlands of southern Ethiopia by members of the Dorze minority ethnic group

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a blue moon, with breathtaking skies, bending palms and waves that accentuate the cool night breeze

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: An exploration of the continent in which Ade examines how African nations may influence international affairs in the next 50 years as life expectancy, population, and economic growth rise. He starts by travelling from Cape Verde to his birthplace in Nigeria, passing through Senegal and Ivory Coast along the way. He discovers how a region once ravaged by the slave trade is still being exploited by Western companies, who dominate the fishing trade, plays a game of para soccer on the streets of Lagos and works with salt collectors at Senegal's Lake Retba

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Afua Hirsch explores three African countries through their art, music and culture, beginning by charting Ethiopia's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr travels the length and breadth of Africa to explore the continent's epic and revelatory history. The first episode examines the origins of human existence and the anthropological and scientific discoveries that point to Africa as the genetic home of all currently living humanity

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr reveals how Christianity and Islam reshaped the landscape and people of Africa between the first and 12th centuries AD and for hundreds of years to come

programme.title: Ted Hughes: Stronger Than Death
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the poet's life shaped his work, including an interview with his daughter Frieda, as well as testimony from family members, friends and fellow writers. The programme explores the passions and impulses behind Hughes's unique poetic voice, which drew on subjects such as the occult, nature, mythology and ancient lore for inspiration, culminating in Birthday Letters, his final volume and only full account of his relationship with wife Sylvia Plath

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the story of sculpture over the past 100 years, a century of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert & George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art form's rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Anthony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a blue moon, with breathtaking skies, bending palms and waves that accentuate the cool night breeze

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Afua Hirsch explores three African countries through their art, music and culture, beginning by charting Ethiopia's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The gravedigger and amateur cameraman profiles the area's countryside by filming its wildlife and people. He begins his exploration by trying to build a badger hide, from which he hopes to observe and record the animals' behaviour over the next 12 months

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a stream trickling through the depths of the forest

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: A morale-boosting stage show offers Captain Blackadder a route out of the trenches, but he soon finds his plans heading for disaster when Gen Melchett falls for the leading lady - Lt George. First World War comedy, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder applies to join the Royal Flying Corps, seeing it as a great way of getting out of the trenches and into a classroom. Comedy, guest starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's biggest white-collar heists, which saw a Malaysian wealth fund looted of $3.5billion and involved government corruption at the highest level, abuse of power and international money laundering. With little to go on, investigative reporters from the New York Times, Wall Street Journal and Hollywood Reporter retrace the dirty money, via real-estate deals and movie financing including The Wolf of Wall Street, back to the top echelons of the Malaysian government. The Prime Minister and his inner circle are implicated, assets are frozen, money is seized and the Malaysian people fight back

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Documentary in which writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith finds out what it took to survive and prosper in the most artistic and decadent royal courts in history. He begins with Richard II, who cultivated the first truly artistic court in England. Painters, sculptors, poets, tailors, weavers and builders flocked there to make their fortunes, but these were dangerous times, and getting close to Richard brought many to a sticky end. Featuring contributions by David Tennant and Clarissa Dickson-Wright

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a stream trickling through the depths of the forest

programme.title: Bunkers, Brutalism and Bloodymindedness: Concrete Poetry with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades concludes a two-part documentary celebrating 20th-century Brutalist architecture, which typically takes the form of block-like concrete constructions. The presenter explores the post-war work of architect Le Corbusier, and argues that buildings once maligned by critics stood for optimism and grandeur

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

With homemade party poppers to make things go with a bang, it’s a party worth celebrating, whoever's it is!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

programme.title: Mystic
programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze’s herd. After some encouragement, Issie decides to ride out on Blaze to find the tracks, and Caroline, Caleb, Dan and Natasha join her on the mission. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they’re completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang’s future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. Despite their best efforts to hide what happened, the consequences of their mission have serious consequences for them all as Tom presents the group with a difficult dilemma. Who will be able to ride in the Cross-Country Trials?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: H and friends look after a runaway egg and find it sitting on a wall

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin has spent days watching his entire film collection and now decided that he wants to make his own serious action film

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Timmy Time

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: A sunbather tries to keep his sun hat on, but the Twirlywoos keep taking it off. Back in the Boat, the Very Important Lady has trouble keeping herself together

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po have a good time rolling a ball down a hill and then rolling each other down it. Then, the Teletubbies enjoy watching children have fun rolling down the slopes too

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with sponges and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Bee has hurt her wing and cannot fly anymore, so the Patchwork Pals must find a way to help her

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Maia has a big family weekend planned. First, she visits her baby cousin Jessica. They enjoy playing peek-a-boo together and Maia even helps to feed her. Later, Dad's family arrive for a traditional Zambian meal. Maia helps prepare the food with her Aunty and then they all dress up in special Zambian clothes to eat

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Animated version of an African folk tale about why moles decided to live underground due to the noise created by other animals

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy gets dirty by jumping into puddles, kicking muddy footballs and getting splattered by jam sandwiches just before the class photograph

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly promises to return a missing doll to its owner, but how can she find out where the owner lives? James helps out, and they make a surprising discovery

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady takes to the skies in a knitted hot-air balloon to race against an old friend

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The words law, lawn, paw and draw are introduced as Alphablock I starts making up new laws

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'mouse' and heads on a whirlwind word adventure. Dee meets lots of different mice - a brown mouse, a furry mouse and a whiskery mouse

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five and friends discover a hole that makes their heads fall off. Learn how to count up and down and how to add to five with the Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: A treasure hunt at a children's birthday party turns into a game of hide-and-seek as the Twirlywoos try to avoid being found. Back in the Boat the Twirlywoos are hidden by strange weeds which grow out of seeds on the floor

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes shoe shopping with his friend

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It's time to wake up but Tinky Winky isn't feeling well. Soon, Tinky Winky feels better but now Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po don't feel well

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Deeno is throwing a fancy dress disco, but there is nothing for Lalloo to wear. They create Lickety Split who helps her make her own sparkly costume, much to her surprise

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo can't decide which shoes to wear, concerned that the others will feel sad. His friends try to help find a solution, such as wearing shoes on his hands and his head

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo explores all his senses, but at the amusement park, he learns that sometimes they can fool people

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Today it is Welsh Day, and the children in reception are celebrating the Welsh language

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Catie is in the driving seat of some amazing machines as she introduces the pre-school audience to some of the biggest, fastest and most amazing machines in the world

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook is laughing so much he almost falls overboard, so Captain Captain insists they learn what to do if one of them actually did fall overboard

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog and the squirrels decide to play hide-and-seek and Duggee finds them all except Roly

programme.title: Peter Rabbit

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels 100 million years back into prehistoric times to meet spinosaurus, a massive meat-eating dinosaur even bigger than a T-Rex. On his adventure he rides a speedy ouranosaurus and takes a dip in a prehistoric river

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how spiders make webs with silk and how watering cans are made in a factory

programme.title: Bitz & Bob

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters master the art of mindfulness to save the Giant Buddha of Leshan in China

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Tim and Dad go to hospital to collect Topsy and Mummy, and Topsy says goodbye to her friend Molly. Later, Tim tells his parents about his failing eyesight

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle ends up in Evie's class, when he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
