programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The duo receive help from a local shop when their bicycle gets a flat tyre

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Colly Wobble has lost her shoes. But in Storyland, Moon Baby shows her a magic shoe tree!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small find a strange silver ball that glows and rings when it bounces. Mother tells them it is a living thing, and Granny is convinced it must have friends nearby

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The friends ride around the garden on the Ninky Nonk. The Tombliboos and Makka Pakka wave to Upsy Daisy and Igglepiggle

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: Scotland v Wales. A look back at the memorable 1997 Five Nations Championship encounter at Murrayfield

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading for the Elan Valley in search of cuckoos. As he soon discovers they are easy to hear but no so easy to see. He also finds red squirrels that are genetically unique to the Cambrians in a remote conifer plantation, and looks for golden plover, one of the rarest breeding birds in Wales, on the hills surrounding Plynlimon

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards focuses on the Industrial Revolution as he continues his exploration of Welsh history. He assesses the impact of the copper trade on the country's landscape, and the importance of iron to Merthyr Tydfil. There is also a look at how the changes sparked social turmoil, riots and uprisings, and led to the first calls for democracy and workers' rights

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat House to follow the lives of the exotic animals at its safari park, beginning by looking at the impact of the pandemic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the aid of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also learns about Captain Matthew Webb, the first person to swim the English Channel

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Political, commercial and romantic rivalries are stirred and Mich's friendship with Sabine might not survive the turmoil

programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Three people compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs, beginning with sausage factory supervisor Mica, bingo host Rob and head gardener Dan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the country lanes of Northern Ireland in their Rolls-Royce. Angus has designs on a piece of Victorian furniture while Roo hits the dance floor

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Victoria Derbyshire investigates the impact of lockdown for those living with an abusive partner, revealing the scale of domestic violence at the height of the crisis and meeting some of those who managed to escape. She asks if the Government has done enough for those who were put in danger by having to stay at home

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Letitia Dean and Kellie Bright, aka Sharon Watts and Linda Carter, to look back at 35 years of Queen Vic landladies, from Angie Watts to Peggy Mitchell. The actresses also revisit their own biggest moments behind the bar, reveal juicy backstage gossip and tease what's to come as Linda hands the landlady reins back to Sharon - all before Stacey puts their landlady knowledge to the ultimate test

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When calls start coming in to report debris on the M4, police discover that a father and son have been in a serious crash. As traffic chaos builds, the pressure is on for the collision team to find out why the car left the road

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates goes on the run, convinced he can save his career and marriage by proving his innocence. As his options dwindle, the anti-corruption team faces renewed pressure to close the case - but rifts start to appear when Arnott questions how far Fleming is willing to go to get results. Police thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Enterprice
programme.desc: As Mustafa's search for his money continues to lead down dead ends, Kazim is busy looking after Jeremiah, who cannot help but reflect on what path he should take next. There are bigger problems for the boys as Mustafa is intent on capturing the Speedi-Kazz team, but help comes from the least expected place

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: Singer-songwriter Allie X is the guest host as the contestants' looks and improv skills are tested, with judges Brooke Lynn Hytes, Jeffrey Bowyer-Chapman and Stacey McKenzie

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast presenter Steph McGovern takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with journalist Ash Sarkar and comedian Josh Widdicombe joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A tiger undergoes surgery, but the vets are soon faced with a difficult decision. A cockatoo ruffles some feathers, and the park's white rhinos enjoy a detoxing charcoal spa

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The new owners of a terraced house in London's Plumstead, a former bakery in Whitstable, Kent, and a three-bedroom semi near Derby city centre reveal their plans for the properties

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits Castell Coch

programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Demands on Max lay him low, and a stolen phone draws Ash and Helena together

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Chris Rogers, Helen Fospero, Ricky Andalcio, Miranda Krestovnikoff and Kevin Duala form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple looking to move to the Norfolk countryside on a budget of £600,000, and volunteers trackside on the world's smallest public railway

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: an estate agent, a street food vendor and a bee farmer compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth traverse south-west Wales, where they learn about traditional boats and sheep farming before attending an auction in Carmarthen

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bonfire night sees fireworks of a different kind in this one-hour special from 2000 which marked the departure of Frank Butcher following the discovery of his affair. As the Vic fills with drunken revellers for Peggy's party, Frank panics when he realises the note he left his wife has gone missing

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Guests Vanessa Williams, Mark Gatiss, Nikkie Tutorials and Denise Van Outen join Michelle for a special episode celebrating her 23rd wedding anniversary, in which we discover how lockdown has affected romance

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a terraced house in Derby, a commercial property near Newcastle-under-Lyme and a bungalow in the Welsh Valleys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Nick Hall help two teams look for items at Nottingham racecourse, while presenter Charlie tests the fairgoers' knowledge of hallmarks

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Trentham Gardens in Staffordshire, discovering how the estate has been a place of Christian worship since the 7th century due to an ancient pilgrimage route

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two expats who want to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is helped by Norman Wilkinson, the Bristol Upholstery Collective and Guy Trench to transform fencing, a pair of demi-johns, a nursing chair and a fire-damaged table

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the extraordinary idea of deploying the Spy Creatures, showing how the concept evolved and became the inspiration for the animatronic animals of the series. This documentary reveals the painstaking work behind building the lifelike models, from first concept until they become 'alive' for the first time. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wrest Park, Bedfordshire, one of the first stately homes in Britain to be converted into a hospital and convalescent home during the First World War. Items brought in to be valued include a diamond and ruby pendant, a rare snuff container, and competing pieces of Daum glassware

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: While Haresh is preoccupied with finding himself a superior job, competition for Lata's hand grows when she returns to Brahmpur and Kabir Durrani re-enters her life. Further complicating matters of the heart, she receives a gift with a romantic message hidden within it. Maan's long-awaited reunion with Saeeda Bai is short-lived when he inadvertently offends her, and he finds himself at the centre of religious tensions and the threat of violence when the festivals of Moharram and Dussehra coincide

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat House to follow the lives of the exotic animals at its safari park, beginning by looking at the impact of the pandemic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood spends 24 hours working as a firefighter in Texas, but just getting the gear on in time is a tough job for him, let alone confronting his fear of heights

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion head to the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the aid of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Anita also learns about Captain Matthew Webb, the first person to swim the English Channel

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple search for a rural home in West Yorkshire with a budget of £500,000, which must have enough space to fit a beloved model railway. The presenter also visits one of the county's heritage fabric mills

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Three people compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs, beginning with sausage factory supervisor Mica, bingo host Rob and head gardener Dan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the country lanes of Northern Ireland in their Rolls-Royce. Angus has designs on a piece of Victorian furniture while Roo hits the dance floor

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps design a plot for Shane Auliffe in Co Kerry, who wants to see a formal, grand garden from his window. He also offers advice with a bit of help from Mount Stewart head gardener Neil Porteous, and shows how to transform a terrace area as he makes the finishing touches to his own garden

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Letitia Dean and Kellie Bright, aka Sharon Watts and Linda Carter, to look back at 35 years of Queen Vic landladies, from Angie Watts to Peggy Mitchell. The actresses also revisit their own biggest moments behind the bar, reveal juicy backstage gossip and tease what's to come as Linda hands the landlady reins back to Sharon - all before Stacey puts their landlady knowledge to the ultimate test

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Victoria Derbyshire investigates the impact of lockdown for those living with an abusive partner, revealing the scale of domestic violence at the height of the crisis and meeting some of those who managed to escape. She asks if the Government has done enough for those who were put in danger by having to stay at home

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates goes on the run, convinced he can save his career and marriage by proving his innocence. As his options dwindle, the anti-corruption team faces renewed pressure to close the case - but rifts start to appear when Arnott questions how far Fleming is willing to go to get results. Police thriller, starring Lennie James, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Frampton: Return of the Jackal
programme.desc: Following Belfast boxer Carl Frampton in the months leading up to one of the biggest nights of his life - a homecoming fight at his beloved Windsor Park. As well as a look at his preparations for the fight, there is a chance to view the former world champion's life away from the ring

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)

programme.title: Sgeulachd Deacon Brodie
programme.desc: John Morrison explores the life of Edinburgh-born William Brodie, whose criminal dealings and double identity is believed to have inspired Robert Louis Stevenson's character Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Tha Donnie Murdo agus Pamaidh ann an Ameireaga còmhla ri Donna Marie, aig àm Taingealachd. Fhad ’s a tha iad an Ameireaga, tha e tighinn am follais gu bheil an t-Ollamh Crògalach a-nis a sàs ann a Hàidreant. Aoighean sònraichte: tha Lindsey Russell ag aithris guth Henrietta agus tha Ben Willbond ag aithris guth an Ollaimh Chrògalaich. Danger Mouse and Penfold get a taste of Thanksgiving when they join Jeopardy Mouse in America. When in America with Jeopardy, they discover that Professor Ham Hands has joined Hydrant. Guest stars: Lindsey Russell as Hysteria and Ben Willbond as Professor Ham Hands

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Bouncing on the trampoline is the latest activity

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary following the activities of Wally, a two-week-old warthog who lives on the African savannah

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Archie and Ben are busy in the lambing shed, Rory goes in search of goose eggs and Morag and Neil are at the cattle sale

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: The history of the London rush hour, featuring montages of the city's traffic lights and the growth in the usage of cars

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Derek Knottenbelt operates on a horse with sarcoids, Fiona Crowden treats a cow that has rapidly lost weight, and Ealasaid Dick is in the midst of lambing

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the world of whistling, including a visit to La Gomera in the Canary Islands where the whistling language is taught in school

programme.title: Piping Live!
programme.desc: Alan MacDonald introduces performances by the Battlefield Band, Ross Miller and Charlie Stewart and Northumberland piper Chris Ormiston

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Hymns, psalms and personal faith stories. Presented by Iain MacKinnon. SIAN sing Glorious Things of Thee are Spoken, while Eilidh Eglinton explains the role faith has in her life

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz, controlling a robot, distributes ice cream to everyone in the city. When Magoo mistakes him for a monster, he gets sprayed with ice cream.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is at the foot of a volcano, but in fact, he's next to a huge soup vat. And he sets it all awry.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz invents a cucumber that turns people into an object of worship. When Magoo sees people being drawn to Fizz and Weasel, he thinks it's zombies!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A rogue AI seizes control of Thunderbird Five, trapping John on board. He tries to alert his brothers of the danger, but the program sends false images back to Tracy Island, leaving them oblivious to his plight

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is peeling off old wallpaper on Linda's walls, but he is actually destroying Fizz' hypnotic posters.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo finds himself shrunk and then dragged inside the President's head, along with Fizz. He thinks the hamster is a little, lost boy and decides to help him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is on a camping trip but he is actually just in the city park. He mistakes Weasel for a wounded bear and tries to help him.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is impressed with the other dogs jumping off a diving board, so he decides to try it himself.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Iris' hat is blown away by a strong wind and lands on the roof, so Mike decides it's time to be a hero.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is invited to lunch at the stately home of an aristocratic lady who isn't all she seems. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben's jealousy has him convinced he has to out-do Kevin.

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. This morning they are joined by Emmerdale actress Lisa Riley and Vamps singer Brad Simpson

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef visits Northumberland, where he is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson. The pair learn how Mead is made on Holy Island, and James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, before they both enjoy a Puffin cruise to Cockett Island

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet conjures up some ideas for Christmas, including baked party canapes, a brussels sprouts tarte tatin and a spectacular chocolate orange profiteroles tower. Liam Charles pays a visit to serve up Christmas lunch on a sheet of ruff puff pastry and Juliet visits chef Chantelle Nicholson in her London restaurant to sample a vegan main course Christmas meal

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three more factories producing some of the nation's favourite food-and-drink items. It reveals the secrets of traditional Lancashire Eccles cakes, using a recipe passed from father to son, jam fit for the Queen's breakfast table and gin infused with exotic natural ingredients at a hi-tech distillery. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher reaches Pembrokeshire, where he meets a family who have moved to the coast to live off-grid and takes part in an archeological dig to unearth the secrets of St Patrick's Chapel. He also joins members of Pembrokeshire Blind Society to find out how they use sound to experience the coast, and sees baby seals at Castlemartin military firing range

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of this year's Battle Rounds in the junior version of the talent contest. Solo singers and duos between the ages of seven and 14 perform to impress judges Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am enough to make them spin their famous red chairs around. There are just six places left in the semi-final up for grabs, bringing the talented performers who win them one step closer to winning a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon oversees the final episode of the singing contest, as the last six mystery celebrities battle it out in while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys in the last small-screen episode, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives

programme.title: Bafta Celebrates Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross hosts a star-studded event at London's Richmond Theatre honouring the ITV drama, originally broadcast ahead of its final episode on Christmas Day 2015. There are reports from behind the scenes with cast and crew to provide an insight into one of the world‘s most popular shows, while creator Julian Fellowes takes to the stage to chat about its origins and secrets. Maggie Smith and Hugh Bonneville perform a specially written sketch, there are performances by Kiri Te Kanawa and Julian Ovenden, and Julie Walters presents the award to Fellowes and the cast and crew

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The first of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Oliver catches his teenage daughter Justina sneaking out of the house to attend a BLM march as he fears for her safety and is concerned she's putting herself at risk. Starring Nicholas Pinnock and Yasmin Monet Prince

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: A middle-class black woman is reunited with her working class white ex-boyfriend at a party. While waiting for a taxi, she talks over the past with him in a way they never had before, and reflect on the role racism and class have played in their relationship. Starring Adelayo Adedayo and Joe Cole

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The third of four short films addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter. Pippa Bennett-Warner and Paapa Essiedu star in Lynette Linton's drama, Look at Me, following the events that unfold when a couple are stopped by police on route to a restaurant, and how the incident impacts their lives

programme.title: Unsaid Stories
programme.desc: The last of four short dramas addressing topics relating to Black Lives Matter centres on an uncomfortable conversation between mixed-race Jordan, who has recently had a baby with a black man, and her mother Lyndsey. As things progress, they are forced to confront and evaluate their relationship as a seemingly immovable wedge appears between them. Written by and starring Nicole Lecky, with Amanda Abbington

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: The soap was founded on its dominating matriarchs and strong northern working-class woman and this edition celebrates the best of them - including the likes of Ena Sharples, Ivy Tilsley, Vera Duckworth, Blanche Hunt and Evelyn Plummer. Comedian Jason Manford narrates the programme that trawls the archives of the soap for its most memorable storylines, moments and characters from the past 60 years, with each episode carefully curated to bring alive each theme via specially selected highlights

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the opening round of the campaign, which came from Donington Park

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Helen Mirren chats to Lorraine Kelly about playing a poodle in new film The One and Only Ivan, as well as supporting the arts and life in lockdown

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and dealers Fay Rutter, David Hakeney and Karen Dalmeny head to Blackburn, Lancashire, where Fay goes round in circles with a bangle, David is charmed by a snake figurine and there's a Russian collection in store for Karen

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Newcastle answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Duncan, Catherine, Jodie and Andy work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone intimidates Dawn and tempts her to take her own life by overdosing on heroin. An anguished Gabby opens up to Leyla about how lonely she feels, and Nate's angry when Al fires him for not attending a meeting

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the day of Gary's wedding, David tells him he needs to make a decision about whether he loves Sarah or Maria. Tim informs Geoff that he and Sally are going away to stay with Gina, and Gemma announces that she's going to set up an online mother and baby group

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As Tracey awaits the results of her scan, she and Sharon swap home truths in the comfort of a spa, and try to make plans for the future, while Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter Naomi. Second of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Frances Ruffelle and Michael Lumsden

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a look at memorable days out and holidays from the soap's archives, revisiting the first episode filmed entirely on location in 1967 and a coach trip to Windermere in 1969 that ended in disaster. Plus, romantic flings of Bet, Rita and Mavis on a girl's holiday to Majorca in 1974

programme.title: The Lost Honour of Christopher Jefferies
programme.desc: The first of a two-part, fact-based drama, starring Jason Watkins. It's December 2010 and retired teacher Christopher Jefferies finds his life in Bristol turned upside down when the police question him about the disappearance of his young tenant Joanna Yeates, who has gone missing just before Christmas. Her murder then becomes a major news story and the press seize upon Jefferies as a suspect, despite a lack of evidence - with destructive results. With Ben Caplan, Shaun Parkes, Nathalie Armin and Joe Sims

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Holly Willoughby welcomes West Ham United footballer Michail Antonio, tennis coach Judy Murray and comedian Joel Dommett to the show. They join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh as well as comics Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen celebrates Diwali in Amritsar, going behind the scenes at a popular destination for traditional sweets, helping a family prepare paneer on an open fire, and watching fireworks over the Golden Temple. Back in the kitchen, she shares a recipe for gajar ka halwa, showcases five unusual ingredients, and gets an introduction to traditional Rajasthani cuisine from chef Vivek Singh

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his den of dealers set up in Northampton. Karen Dalmeny meets her knight in shining armour, Jo Brayshaw hits the right notes, while Henry Nicholls is served up a right royal dilemma

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Wales answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: The Lost Honour of Christopher Jefferies
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama based on the murder of landscape architect Joanna Yeates. The last thing Christopher Jefferies wants after being vilified by the national press is to return to the limelight. However, as it becomes apparent things won't be going back to normal any time soon, the retired teacher embarks on a fight to clear his name. Starring Jason Watkins, with Ben Caplan, Shaun Parkes, Nathalie Armin and Joe Sims

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: David Dickinson and dealers Fay Rutter, David Hakeney and Karen Dalmeny head to Blackburn, Lancashire, where Fay goes round in circles with a bangle, David is charmed by a snake figurine and there's a Russian collection in store for Karen

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Newcastle answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Duncan, Catherine, Jodie and Andy work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone intimidates Dawn and tempts her to take her own life by overdosing on heroin. An anguished Gabby opens up to Leyla about how lonely she feels, and Nate's angry when Al fires him for not attending a meeting

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the day of Gary's wedding, David tells him he needs to make a decision about whether he loves Sarah or Maria. Tim informs Geoff that he and Sally are going away to stay with Gina, and Gemma announces that she's going to set up an online mother and baby group

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As Tracey awaits the results of her scan, she and Sharon swap home truths in the comfort of a spa, and try to make plans for the future, while Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter Naomi. Second of a two-part episode of the comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Frances Ruffelle and Michael Lumsden

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a look at memorable days out and holidays from the soap's archives, revisiting the first episode filmed entirely on location in 1967 and a coach trip to Windermere in 1969 that ended in disaster. Plus, romantic flings of Bet, Rita and Mavis on a girl's holiday to Majorca in 1974

programme.title: The Lost Honour of Christopher Jefferies
programme.desc: The first of a two-part, fact-based drama, starring Jason Watkins. It's December 2010 and retired teacher Christopher Jefferies finds his life in Bristol turned upside down when the police question him about the disappearance of his young tenant Joanna Yeates, who has gone missing just before Christmas. Her murder then becomes a major news story and the press seize upon Jefferies as a suspect, despite a lack of evidence - with destructive results. With Ben Caplan, Shaun Parkes, Nathalie Armin and Joe Sims

Back in the kitchen, she shares a recipe for gajar ka halwa, showcases five unusual ingredients, and gets an introduction to traditional Rajasthani cuisine from chef Vivek Singh

programme.title: The Lost Honour of Christopher Jefferies
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama based on the murder of landscape architect Joanna Yeates. The last thing Christopher Jefferies wants after being vilified by the national press is to return to the limelight. However, as it becomes apparent things won't be going back to normal any time soon, the retired teacher embarks on a fight to clear his name. Starring Jason Watkins, with Ben Caplan, Shaun Parkes, Nathalie Armin and Joe Sims

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Saint's bones are discovered during an archaeological dig, causing a stir in the village of Midsomer Cicely. However, when the dig's leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are presented with a complex investigation as they hunt for the perpetrator. As the duo unearth more evidence, they soon discover that it is not only skeletons that have been long buried in the hallowed ground. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk, Fiona Dolman and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Divorced couple Marion Williams and James McDonnell come together to search for the child they were forced to give up for adoption in the face of overwhelming family opposition when they were teenagers. Elsewhere, Cathie Cutler-Evans searches for her birth mother, Adrienne Powell, who had to give her up after being unable to offer Cathie a stable home as an unmarried 19-year-old

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: The arrival of Miss Lambe's forbidden love causes Charlotte to question her opinion of Sidney. Clara witnesses a moment between Esther and Sir Edward that raises her suspicions about their relationship, with devastating consequences for Esther. Period drama, starring Rose Williams and Theo James

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: George refuses to apologise for his marriage to Amelia and his father sends him a letter, cutting him off from the family and denying him his inheritance. All the friends set sail for Belgium, where Becky makes use of every opportunity to advance in society, laying on the charm with the great and the good. Even George is captivated by Becky's new glamour, as Amelia notices with mounting dread. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manoeuvres her way to the top of Georgian high society. Starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a teenager with severe learning difficulties is killed in a fire at his home, Brooks and Devlin's investigation leads them to the last person they thought could be responsible. Matters are further complicated when Steel and Phillips come up against brash defence lawyer Dominic Peck, whose win-at-all-costs attitude and outrageous ego threaten the fair outcome of the trial. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny's younger brother Steve pays a visit, and soon begins to threaten her relationship with Merton. Meanwhile, David sets his sights on joining a mod gang causing havoc in the village. Sarah Tansey, Thom Fell, Maureen O'Brien and Robert East star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The penny finally drops for Leyla, Dawn makes a discovery, and Lydia has her suspicions

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma feels Mike cares more for the business than her. Jack feels Terry is only visiting because of their inheritance money. Norris is reconciled with Angela

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera wants to invite Terry to stay permanently, and he is interested to learn Jack has shares in a racehorse. Don is furious to hear Josie has resigned

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dee is sad that her family cannot attend the wedding. Biff and Linda enjoy a night away in a hotel for Linda's birthday. Zak, Barry and Lisa get drunk together in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles check the damage caused by the rocket explosion, Zak is upset by how much time Lisa is spending with Barry, and Frank helps DI Cooke with her investigations

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Little Birchmarch is besieged by traffic until Ida's novel approach makes the Highway's Chief act

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina Ward makes a surprise return to Aidensfield and Bellamy encounters a key informant who might come in useful - but finds himself in trouble when the man disappears before he can offer a tip-off about an imminent armed robbery. Meanwhile, Vernon acquires a bargain miniature railway which he hopes will impress the new estate manager, and it emerges Dr Liz Merrick may have feelings for Lord Ashfordly's recently appointed land agent. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is not allowed to see Frank at the police station, and he is formally charged with Kim's murder. Eric holds his after hours poker game in the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank appears in court and Chris is sure he will get bail. Zoe is distraught when the judge delivers the verdict. Vic is pleased when Viv seems upset about his date with Kathy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie confesses to Don that Mike set them up. Terry is annoyed to discover that all the inheritance went into the pub. Tricia gets drunk in the Rovers and lays into Stephen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Terry does not like the way Vera quizzes him about his private life. Don lays into Mike, accusing him of setting him up with a duff business

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina Ward makes a surprise return to Aidensfield and Bellamy encounters a key informant who might come in useful - but finds himself in trouble when the man disappears before he can offer a tip-off about an imminent armed robbery. Meanwhile, Vernon acquires a bargain miniature railway which he hopes will impress the new estate manager, and it emerges Dr Liz Merrick may have feelings for Lord Ashfordly's recently appointed land agent. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie and Chris clash over looking after James. Kelly is excited for Tom's return. Zoe tells Chris she is taking over running Home Farm Estates at Frank's request

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak does not think the thugs will stop harassing Mandy. Chris resents Zoe taking over the business. Vic tells Viv about Terry chatting up Helen Ackroyd

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly tries to boost Raquel's confidence. Josie is upset that she and Don are splitting up. Andy visits Steve on their birthday and feels sorry for him in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don feels he is better off without Josie. Vera phones her solicitor to get Terry cut out of her will. Raquel falls out with Curly and tells him she's entitled to her own work

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Imran that he's convinced Gary murdered Rick. Alya confides in Imran that she will sell her share of Speed Daal to distance herself from Geoff. And Karen the social worker promises Toyah she will put the fostering wheels in motion once she's received the reference from Leanne

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah admits to Adam that if Gary did kill Rick then he did it to protect her family. Realising the stress of Oliver's condition is getting to Steve, she offers a shoulder to cry on. And when Geoff refuses to let Alya buy him out of Speed Daal, she takes great delight in dropping a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fears that Sarah has shared his darkest secrets, Maria asks David to witness her wedding instead of a sick Audrey, and a spiteful Geoff tells Sally that Tim will always choose him over her

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Leyla has an intruder in her office, Jamie confesses to Belle, and Mandy asks for Lydia's help

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is no going back for Dawn, Nate suggests he skip work to support Tracy, and Harriet feels more guilty than ever

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Meanwhile, Vernon acquires a bargain miniature railway which he hopes will impress the new estate manager, and it emerges Dr Liz Merrick may have feelings for Lord Ashfordly's recently appointed land agent. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is not allowed to see Frank at the police station, and he is formally charged with Kim's murder. Eric holds his after hours poker game in the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank appears in court and Chris is sure he will get bail. Zoe is distraught when the judge delivers the verdict. Vic is pleased when Viv seems upset about his date with Kathy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie confesses to Don that Mike set them up. Terry is annoyed to discover that all the inheritance went into the pub. Tricia gets drunk in the Rovers and lays into Stephen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Terry does not like the way Vera quizzes him about his private life. Don lays into Mike, accusing him of setting him up with a duff business

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Unable to get used to the fact she was second choice for the sergeant's job, Rachel punishes Janet for keeping the truth from her, and although Will cheers her up with the news she has been chosen for a vice initiative, the detective is soon stung by Gill's harsh words. Meanwhile, an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital with injuries that do not match his parents' explanation of events, and when an unregistered childminder and her boyfriend enter the equation, it's unclear who had the boy's best interests at heart

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis
